comments.body: It is better to use Eclipse
It Is even better to use the free Community Edition of IntelliJ.

answers.body:
Preface

If you want to develop with Java, use IntelliJ IDEA for this.

You will never get happy with VSC for Java.

Solution

Step 1: Download the free version of IntelliJ IDEA.

Direct Link for Windows: https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/download/download-thanks.html?platform=windows&code=IIC

Direct Link for Linux: https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/download/download-thanks.html?platform=linux&code=IIC

Other versions such MacOS: https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/download/?section=windows

Step 2: Install and get happy

Everything will work out of the box and will be preconfigured perfectly fine for you.

I've been working with Java for quite some time now and I started with VSCode and sometimes use both for certain practices. But overall I'm happy with VSCode. And today I ran into a similar issue with the Java extensions. I had 'that' same issue(the running infinitely) and another, that none of the 'New Java File' / Package / Project options were working.

My fix was to use this built-in repair command: Java: Clean Java Language Server Workspace after pressing Ctrl+Shift+P in VSCode. That fixed both issues.

Also in my search for a solution I saw this following tip(but must say that before today I had no issues with my big folder with many projects in it):

Try to avoid having very large folders open in VS Code (like the entire C: drive or your user home directory). The Java language server tries to index everything it sees — and that’s usually how it gets stuck.

Hope that helps.

title: VSCode Java extension isn't running
body:
enter image description here

Java extension for vscode just analyzes for something infinitely, so I can't run my project. Before it worked properly and I didn't change anything

I did the following:

  • cleaned java lsp (language server) and shared indexes
  • switched to release extension version from pre release
  • reloaded window, IDE and OS after actions above
